assertions.text: Output contains a markdown table (using | column | syntax)
assertions.evidence: | Scenario | Applicant Age | Credit Score | Income Status | Min Score (Policy) | Approved? | Rejection Reason? |

assertions.text: At least one output column name ends with '?' (e.g. 'Eligible?' or 'Approved?')
assertions.evidence: Column headers include 'Approved?' and 'Rejection Reason?' — both end with question marks

assertions.text: Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'creditScore', 'isEligible', 'boolean', 'int'
assertions.evidence: Columns are: 'Scenario', 'Applicant Age', 'Credit Score', 'Income Status', 'Min Score (Policy)', 'Approved?', 'Rejection Reason?' — all business/domain language, no camelCase implementation terms

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row specifically for the senior applicant (65+) threshold scenario
assertions.evidence: | Senior: score at senior threshold | 65 | 600 | Stable | 600 | **?** | Same ambiguity — ≥600 or >600? | and multiple other senior rows (age 70, 65, 64→65)

assertions.text: The missing income scenario is represented — either as a row with an open/uncertain expected value (?, TBD, or blank) or as an explicit open question note
assertions.evidence: | Missing income information | 40 | 700 | Unknown | 650 | **?** | Open — see below | and explicit section '**2. What happens when income information is missing?**'

assertions.text: Scenario names describe conditions ('Senior applicant at threshold') not outcomes ('Approved')
assertions.evidence: Two scenario names use outcome language: 'Standard approval' and 'Senior approval using lower threshold' — both use the word 'approval' which is an outcome, not a description of input conditions. Condition-focused names should avoid outcome terms (e.g., 'Standard applicant with credit score above threshold' instead of 'Standard approval')

assertions.text: The policy thresholds (650 for standard, 600 for senior) appear as concrete values in the Credit Score column — not just described in prose. The reader can see the boundary values in the table rows.
assertions.evidence: Credit Score column contains explicit rows with 650 (| Credit score at standard threshold | 40 | 650 |...) and 600 (| Senior: score at senior threshold | 65 | 600 |...) as concrete boundary values
